summary: Stunning audience recording of a high‑energy show featuring a smoking Shakedown Street, Samson and the unforgettable Neville Brothers guest spot.
review: The tape captures one of the most celebrated late‑80s Dead shows – Jerry’s “comeback” vibe, a blistering second set with Shakedown Street, Samson, Iko‑Iko, Day‑O and a soaring Knockin’ on Heaven’s Door, all amplified by the Neville Brothers.  Reviewers rave about the warm bass, crystal‑clear crowd sing‑along and superb balance, calling it the best AUD of this era (with only a wish for a hybrid mix).

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high 4‑5 star reviews praise both the musical performance (standout second set, guest appearance) and the audience recording quality (warm bass, excellent balance).

recording_quality.technical_notes: Warm, full‑bodied bass; clear vocal and instrumental separation; crowd singing captured vividly; reviewers note superb balance between band and audience. No major drop‑outs reported, only a desire for a future hybrid mix.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A solid first set followed by an explosive second set that “cooked” with guest Neville Brothers, high‑energy jams and emotional moments (Jerry’s comeback vibe, the moving Knockin’ on Heaven’s Door).

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for his ecstatic voice, strong guitar work and even a brief skat singing moment; described as a ‘comeback party’.
band_member_comments.Keys: Mentions Brent (Mydland) in the context of 1980s shows; no specific keyboard performance notes.
